summary: Improvement cycle addressing external reviewer feedback (berendt review #2). Five WARNING items and two AUTO_FIX INFO items were correctly fixed: (W-001) the upgrade-flow runbook now uses a literal ISO 8601 date instead of GNU-only `date -d '7 days ago'`, with a comment explaining the rationale; (W-002) the bypass-log assertion now matches the structured-key form `"namespace"="default"` / `"name"="test-keystone"` rather than a substring that collided with the message text; (W-003) the bypass branch now emits a real `corev1.EventTypeWarning` Event of reason `TrustFlushBypass` on the Keystone CR via `r.Recorder.Event`, the test was renamed from `LogsWarning` to `EmitsWarningEvent` and now also asserts the Event via the existing `expectEvent` helper, and the reconciler doc was updated to describe both the structured log AND the Warning Event; (W-004) the self-negating bullet in keystone-upgrade-flow.md was replaced with a callout `Note:` block; (I-001) the in-function comments now read `Production path` / `Legacy bypass` instead of inverted Path 1/Path 2 numbering; (I-003) the bypass-branch comment now acknowledges that programmatic callers (envtest/unit tests) also reach this branch, not only webhook-misconfigured clusters. (I-002) the hourly-tick race in the chainsaw suspend test was fixed via a managedFields-based SUSPEND_TIME extraction that filters Jobs to those created strictly after the suspend patch — robust and correct. Trust-flush unit tests pass; `go vet` is clean across the keystone module.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./operators/keystone/... → ok)
Bypass-log assertion now validates structured keys, not message-text substring (W-002)
New event-emission assertion via expectEvent helper (W-003)
Chainsaw timestamp-based filter eliminates the hourly-tick race (I-002)
Renamed test name accurately reflects what it tests (W-003)

code_quality_checklist.item: Recorder.Event call follows the existing pattern in reconcile_database.go
Comments and godoc match the implementation (production path / legacy bypass)
No dead code or scope creep
No new abstractions or wrappers introduced unnecessarily

security_checklist.item: No new attack surface (warning Event is internal-only)
No secrets in event message or log

architecture_checklist.item: Bypass posture surfaced via two complementary channels (log + Event)
Reuses existing event-emission pattern (Kubernetes Event alongside condition-setting)
Path naming inversion resolved without numeric ordering

dry_yagni_checklist.item: expectEvent helper reused instead of building a new assertion path
Recorder field already on KeystoneReconciler — no new wiring needed

fail_fast_checklist.item: Chainsaw script exits 1 with diagnostic dump if SUSPEND_TIME cannot be derived
Doc snippet uses literal date — fails loudly if date is not updated, instead of silently mis-flushing

defensive_checklist.item: jq filter handles missing ownerReferences via `(.metadata.ownerReferences // [])`
Bypass branch uses Recorder.Event (no panic risk — Recorder is always wired in production and in test reconciler)

suggested_improvements: (non-blocking, AC-only) W-005 from the external review is not addressed in code — the brownfield-upgrade acceptance criterion in the PR description still claims materialization happens 'within one reconcile' on upgrade, while the implementation correctly relies on a CREATE/UPDATE admission round-trip. Consider rewording the AC in the PR body or the planwerk progress JSON to match the documented reality (or open a follow-up issue for controller-driven materialization). Not a code defect.
next_steps: Reword W-005 acceptance criterion in PR description to match implemented behavior, or scope a follow-up issue for controller-driven materialization on first reconcile
Run the chainsaw trust-flush suite once on a kind cluster to confirm the managedFields timestamp extraction works against the live API server (only static review possible in sandbox)
